Title: A combinatorial action of GmMYB176 and GmbZIP5 controls isoflavonoid biosynthesis in soybean (Glycine max)

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin-mediated plant defense; rhizobial chemoattraction; human health-promoting bioactivity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R1 MYB transcription factor; SHAQKYF-type R1 MYB
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmCHS8; multiple genes in the isoflavonoid biosynthetic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates GmCHS8 expression and regulates multiple isoflavonoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimer; interacts with GmbZIP5 and GmbZIP4; previously reported to interact with SGF14 proteins (14-3-3) for nucleocytoplasmic shuttling
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmbZIP5; GmbZIP4; GmbHLH5; GmbHLH15; SGF14 proteins; GmMYB176
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphorylation state at Ser29 affects interaction partners and localization behavior
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: necessary but not sufficient activator of isoflavonoid biosynthesis; requires GmbZIP5 co-action for strong activation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression in hairy roots using pK7WG2D-derived construct; specific promoter NA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: soybean hairy root system; phytoalexins discussed in stress/defense context but core validation without external elicitor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; yeast one-hybrid; RNAi context from partner gene; co-overexpression in hairy roots; qRT-PCR; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-overexpression with GmbZIP5 increases total isoflavonoids and elevates glyceollin, isowighteone, and O-methylhydroxy isoflavone-related metabolites in soybean hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: alters metabolite production beyond phenylpropanoid/isoflavonoid pathway based on prior transcriptomic/metabolomic study; no growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: stack or co-express with GmbZIP5 to enhance soybean root isoflavonoids and phytoalexins for defense and nutritional/health value; GmMYB176 alone is insufficient
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (Co-IP LC-MS/MS), PRIDE PXD023931; Metabolomics LC-MS, Metabolomics Workbench ST001634; tissue RNA-seq data extracted from Phytozome
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB176 physically interacts with GmbZIP5 and GmbZIP4 in planta and binds the critical 30-bp GmCHS8 promoter fragment. 2. GmMYB176 is necessary but alone insufficient to elevate isoflavonoids in soybean hairy roots. 3. Co-overexpression of GmMYB176 with GmbZIP5 markedly increases multiple isoflavonoid phytoalexins, establishing GmMYB176-GmbZIP5 as a functional regulatory complex.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phospho-mutant GmMYB176S29A shows stronger interaction with GmbZIP5/GmbZIP4 and retains DNA binding, suggesting unphosphorylated form may act as activator; however exact active phosphorylation state in transcriptional complex remains unresolved

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin-mediated plant defense; rhizobial chemoattraction; human health-promoting bioactivity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation in combinatorial complex with GmMYB176
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmCHS8 promoter 30-bp fragment; likely GmCHS8 and downstream isoflavonoid biosynthesis program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: acts positively on isoflavonoid biosynthetic gene activation together with GmMYB176
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with GmMYB176 in planta; proposed GmMYB176-GmbZIP5 transcriptional complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: critical interacting partner required for effective activation of isoflavonoid biosynthesis in soybean roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi by pK7GWIWG2D(II); overexpression fusion in pK7WG2D; specific promoter NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-IP candidate identification; BiFC; yeast one-hybrid; RNAi silencing in hairy roots; translational fusion co-overexpression with GmMYB176; qRT-PCR; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi silencing decreases total isoflavonoids, while co-overexpression with GmMYB176 increases total isoflavonoids and strongly elevates glyceollin, isowighteone, and O-methylhydroxy isoflavone-related metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising regulator for engineering higher soybean isoflavonoids/phytoalexins, especially in combination with GmMYB176
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (Co-IP LC-MS/MS), PRIDE PXD023931; Metabolomics LC-MS, Metabolomics Workbench ST001634; tissue RNA-seq data extracted from Phytozome
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmbZIP5 was identified as a GmMYB176 interactor by Co-IP and validated in planta by BiFC. 2. GmbZIP5 binds the critical GmCHS8 promoter fragment in yeast one-hybrid assays. 3. RNAi silencing of GmbZIP5 reduces total isoflavonoid accumulation, and co-overexpression with GmMYB176 strongly enhances isoflavonoid phytoalexins in soybean hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested interactors, GmbZIP5 but not GmbZIP4 showed clear functional impact on isoflavonoid accumulation despite both binding the promoter fragment and interacting with GmMYB176

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytoalexin-mediated plant defense; rhizobial chemoattraction; human health-promoting bioactivity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding and protein interaction with GmMYB176, but functional contribution not supported by metabolite phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: candidate positive regulator of GmCHS8, not functionally established
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi by pK7GWIWG2D(II); overexpression fusion in pK7WG2D; specific promoter NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-IP candidate identification; BiFC; yeast one-hybrid; RNAi silencing; translational fusion overexpression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Despite promoter binding and interaction with GmMYB176, silencing or co-overexpression did not significantly alter total isoflavonoid levels in soybean hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited current value for isoflavonoid engineering based on lack of metabolite phenotype in this study
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (Co-IP LC-MS/MS), PRIDE PXD023931; tissue RNA-seq data extracted from Phytozome
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmbZIP4 interacts with GmMYB176 and binds the GmCHS8 promoter fragment. 2. GmbZIP4 is co-expressed with GmMYB176 in roots. 3. Functional assays did not show a significant effect of GmbZIP4 on total isoflavonoid accumulation, so it is not supported as a core causal regulator here.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested in results, but evidence does not support a causal metabolite effect comparable to GmbZIP5; users may exclude it from strict core set if requiring positive causal effect only
